subject: Project Management
title: The Product Owner
description: A quick, practical guide to The Product Owner.

hook.text: The Product Owner is the single voice of the customer inside the Scrum team.

buildup.text: They decide what to build, in what order, and define what 'done' looks like for each feature.

discovery.text: The backlog is their tool — a prioritized list of everything the product could become.

twist.text: Many organizations split the role across committees — destroying the single-voice advantage.

climax.text: A great Product Owner says 'no' more than 'yes.' Focus is their superpower.

punchline.text: The hardest job in Scrum: deciding what not to build.

quiz.question: What is the Product Owner's main responsibility?
options: Deciding what to build and in what order
